posts.author: Pierce007 (Rune Haarberg)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have made an automation from Monday to Outlook calendar (when an item is created or updated , create an item in Outlook and sync future chandgs from this board).
When Im in Outlook calendar and change due date - it wanrt sync in Monday. How can I do this, without having to use Monday?

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Pierce007 - make sure you are adding both Outlook Calendar Integrations (monday > Outlook and Outlook > monday) to enable the two-way sync. This help article does a good job of walking though the setup process.

Outlook Calendar Integration – Support

Hope this helps!
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Pierce007.

We developed an app called Outlook Calendar Integration Plus that provides a robust Outlook calendar <> monday.com integration.

Here are the key differences from monday’s native solution -

  1. Allows to select which calendar to use (not just the primary default)
  2. Supports subitems
  3. Much more flexible triggers - “no more sync every item”
  4. Allows full mapping - updates, invitees and more
  5. Allows to control the start & end date and time.

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - Outlook Calendar Integration Plus - monday.com App Marketplace

Hope that helps
